resources.string: Add a server profile in Settings to begin.
Type a message below to start chatting.
Tap the plus button to start a new chat.
Session token returned by the server
Keeps the app alive while Claude streams an answer.
Notifies when Claude has finished replying.
Stream is running in the background
Uses the OAuth session of the locally-installed `claude` CLI on the server. Runs against your Claude Pro or Max subscription quota. No further setup needed.
Uses a direct Anthropic Console API key. Pay-as-you-go billing against console.anthropic.com.
Routes through Amazon Bedrock with your AWS credentials. Required for organizations that bill via AWS. Supports Claude Opus 4.7 on Bedrock-pinned model IDs.
Pro/Max plans bill flat-rate — counts here are informational only.
After ~6 lines your input is cut off with a "Show more" toggle. Saves scrolling when reading long replies. Tap the bubble to expand.
Personal chat front-end for your Pocket Claude server.
↑ Very old history was condensed (emergency compaction)
This chat has its own skill settings that differ from your default.
Default settings from Settings are active. Changes here only override for this chat.
Should Pocket Claude read replies in this chat automatically?
You need a Google AI Studio API key to generate images (this runs through Gemini Nano Banana, not through Claude). Add your key in Settings under "Image generation".
No images generated yet. Type a prompt above and tap "Generate".
Removes this entry from your history. The image files on the server are kept.
Removes all entries from the image history. The image files on the server are kept.
Describe the image — e.g. "Cyberpunk cat in neon rain"
After ~6 lines your input is cut off with a "Show more" toggle. Saves scrolling when reading long replies. Tap the bubble to expand.
Not signed in — please sign in below.
How is my phone connected to the server?
Each profile is its own server endpoint with its own username. You can keep multiple profiles in parallel (e.g. home + on the go) and switch between them in the list below.
When the server runs on your Mac it usually starts a Cloudflare Quick Tunnel with a URL like https://abc-xyz-123.trycloudflare.com. Within your home Wi-Fi http://<Mac-IP>:8787 also works.
First-time sign-in uses the server admin\'s initial password. The server then forces you to pick your own. No API key on the phone.
How much Claude reasons internally before answering. Higher = better quality for hard questions, longer wait. `xhigh` is Opus 4.7 only and sits between `high` and `max`.

Standard = Anthropic default like on claude.ai. Permissive = neutral adult-to-adult variant with less hedging. Ultra-Liberal = minimal refusal layer, only hard limits (minor safety, illegal synthesis/malware). Custom = entirely your own.
Which tools may Claude use — WebSearch, WebFetch, code execution. These are defaults for new chats. Per-chat override via the ⋮ menu in the chat top right.
Pocket Claude can read Claude\'s replies aloud. Three providers are available — from "zero setup" to "premium voice quality".
Microsoft Edge reading voices. Completely free, no API key or service account. Quality is more mechanical than Gemini/Chirp but perfect to try TTS instantly.
Free Tier: 10 calls/day per key — tight with 1 key, good with a multi-key pool. Paid: $0.50/M input + $10/M audio output. Best model inference.
1M characters/month FREE on Chirp 3 HD (≈ 20 h of audio). Best voice quality. Setup more complex (Cloud project + service account JSON).
For image generation you need an API key from Google AI Studio. It\'s stored server-side per profile and can optionally be reused for the Gemini voice output.
https://aistudio.google.com → sign in with your Google account.
Left side "API keys" → "Create API key". Keys start with "AIza…".
Paste the key into the field below.
Possible without a credit card (select "Create in new project" when generating the key). 15 calls/min, 1,500 calls/day.
Higher rate limits, no training. Nano-Banana ≈ $0.04/image.
💡 The same key also works for Gemini TTS speech output (section "Read aloud"). If no separate key is configured there, the server uses this one automatically.
All conversations + attachments as an encrypted ZIP (AES-256). On import: "Replace" overwrites, "Merge" combines.
TTS provider, the full API-key pool, image API key, skills, voice, theme, effort, system prompt — as JSON. WARNING: API keys are in PLAINTEXT, treat the file like a password.
Neither bundle contains profiles (server URL, username, token) — those stay per device.
Personal chat front-end for your Pocket Claude server.
Bash/Python in server sandbox for computations.

A pool of multiple AI Studio keys multiplies the daily TTS limit. The server distributes TTS calls round-robin with rate limiting across all keys — no single key gets hammered, free tier holds longer.
Free tier per key: 10 RPD on TTS preview models (3 RPM rolling). For a chat app with chunking that\'s very generous.
Tip: 2–3 keys from different Google accounts is enough (one per account possible — the tier sits on the project, not the account). Each key separately on the free tier, no credit card needed.
Your own Google AI Studio API key. Stored just for you.
Get a key at aistudio.google.com/apikey. Image generation needs the project to be set up — "Create in new project" works automatically.
You see an ESTIMATE of how much you spent on Cloud TTS this month. It comes from the server-internal character counter × voice price per 1M.
Microsoft Edge reading voices, completely free — no API key, no service account. Quality weaker than Gemini/Chirp but fine for quick reading.
Easy on the monthly free quota. Enable chunking below to parallelize long passages and keep latency reasonable.
If a long answer responds slowly: try enabling chunking (below) — it brings the "time to first audio" down a lot on Cloud TTS.
Free Tier per key runs out quickly. Add more keys below for more throughput.
because the first chunk already plays while the others are being generated
the daily budget. With a large multi-key pool you can
Pitch stability and emotional range vary by model.
Current default. Very good quality, cheapest.
Pro model, slower + pricier. Only pick if the extra cost actually sounds better.
Paste the contents of the Google Cloud service-account JSON file here.
Export all chats from the server as a ZIP (for Google Drive, Telegram …)
Replace = current server state is overwritten completely with the backup contents. Merge = only chats that don\'t already exist here are added.
Optional: password for AES-256 encryption. If you store the backup on a remote service, this protects you against access. Leave empty for an unencrypted ZIP.
This backup was protected with a password. Please enter it.
Sign in to your server account (below)
Read-aloud — optional, Edge default needs no setup
Microsoft Edge\'s reading voices. Completely free, no API key, no service account. Quality weaker than Gemini/Chirp but fine for fast read-aloud.
API key from Google AI Studio. Free Tier: 10 calls/day per key — very limited with one key, usable with a multi-key pool. Paid: $0.50/1M input + $10/1M audio output. Best model inference.
Service account JSON from Google Cloud. 1M chars/month free (~20h audio) with Chirp 3 HD. Best voice-quality range, more complex setup (Cloud project + service account).
Long replies are split into chunks and sent to the TTS API in parallel. Audio starts faster because the first chunk plays while the others are still being synthesized.
Recommended ON — no real rate limit, faster stream start with no downsides.

Default OFF. Each chunk = one API call. With free-tier keys (10 RPD/key) a chunked answer quickly burns through your daily budget. With a large multi-key pool you can enable it manually — the server will then round-robin chunks across all keys.
✓ Edge TTS active — free, no setup. Quality is standard (Microsoft Edge voices). Switch later to Gemini API or Cloud TTS for higher quality.

✓ %1$d keys in pool, chunking ON. Each reply is split into multiple parallel chunks — they are round-robin routed across all keys. Effective burst: ≈ %2$d parallel TTS calls (3 RPM per key). Fastest stream start but burns your daily budget fast.
✓ %1$d keys in pool, chunking OFF. Each reply uses exactly one key (round-robin). No burst load. Gentle on the daily budget. Turn chunking on below if you want faster stream start.
✓ Gemini API with 1 TTS key. Free Tier: 3 RPM / 10 RPD per key. With chunking ON (below) a long answer quickly burns the daily budget. Add more keys below for more throughput.
ⓘ The image API key is currently used for TTS as well. Add a separate free-tier key (or several) below so TTS and image quotas don\'t compete.
⚠ No API key set. Paste your AI Studio key into the field below (free tier is enough — see ⓘ next to the field).
✓ Cloud TTS active + chunking ON. 1M chars/month free (Chirp 3 HD), no relevant RPM brake — chunking gives faster stream start without downsides.
✓ Cloud TTS active. 1M chars/month free (Chirp 3 HD). You could enable chunking (below) — it gives faster stream start without quota risk.
⚠ No service-account JSON loaded. Admin needs to upload one in the server GUI (Cloud project with active billing).
Please save the server URL and token first, then you can upload the TTS JSON.
As soon as Claude replies, playback starts — handy in the car or on the move.
The model decides the generation of the Gemini TTS engine. Voices sound different by model — pacing, pitch stability and emotional range vary.
Current default. Very good quality, cheapest.
Newer generation, livelier. Free tier limit: 10 calls/day/key.
Pro model, slower + pricier. Only pick if the extra cost actually sounds better.
Paste the contents of the Google Cloud service-account JSON file here. You create it at console.cloud.google.com → IAM → Service accounts.
Export all chats from the server as a ZIP (for Google Drive, Telegram …) or import an existing backup here.
Replace = current server state is overwritten completely (an internal backup is saved automatically beforehand).\n\nMerge = only chats that don\'t already exist here are added.
Please briefly restart the server (in the server manager on the mini-PC) so all DB connections use the new file.
No profile yet — enter URL + token below and a "Default" profile will be created automatically.
Profile "%1$s" is removed locally. The data on the server stays intact — you can sign in again with the same username at any time.
When you first sign in you have to set a new password.
All settings + API keys as a file. Profiles are excluded.
✓ Import successful: %1$d server settings applied, %2$d API keys imported.
Add multiple AI Studio API keys here. The server distributes TTS calls round-robin with rate limiting — this bypasses the per-project limits of the free tier.

Live test May 2026: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS preview free tier has only 10 requests per DAY per project. Gemini 2.5 Flash TTS preview is more generous (3 RPM rolling). With chunking that gets tight. With 3-5 keys (from separate projects) you scale linearly.
How do you get multiple keys?

On aistudio.google.com create a NEW project per key (works within the same Google account — the tier sits on the project, not the account). Or use a separate Google account. Both work, both are free tier, no credit card needed.
The server tracks per-key recent calls (sliding window), 2 RPM per key as a safe limit below the 3 RPM cap. For a chunk call it picks the key with the most free slots.

If you don\'t want to manage multiple keys: use Cloud TTS (service account, 1M chars/month free, no RPM limit) OR a paid-tier Gemini key (one key is enough, covered by your $10 AI-Pro credit + 8€ hard cap).
No keys in pool yet. Add the first one below.
%1$d key%2$s in pool — estimated %3$d calls/min throughput.
Your own Google AI Studio API key. Stored just for you.
No API key — image mode is disabled.
Get a key at aistudio.google.com/apikey. For image generation the Cloud project needs a billing account (free tier covers only text).
Microphone → Groq Whisper Large v3 Turbo

Tap the mic in the chat input to record. When you tap again the audio goes to Groq\'s Whisper Large v3 Turbo and the transcript lands in the text field. Long-press the mic during a recording to cancel without sending.
Free tier at console.groq.com — sign up, create an API key, paste it below. Keys start with “gsk_”. Whisper Large v3 Turbo is free under the standard rate limits.
The transcription prompt is picked from the active app language so punctuation, capitalization and tech terms come out the way you write.
In the chat overflow (⋮) menu. After recording the message sends, the reply is read aloud, and the mic starts again — for hands-free back-and-forth. Tap the “Auto-mode active” pill to stop the loop.
Your own Groq API key. Stored only for you, never shared.
Get a key at console.groq.com/keys. Free tier covers Whisper Large v3 Turbo with rate limits suitable for personal use.
By default Whisper follows the app language. Pick \"Manual\" to override — for languages we don\'t pre-ship, the bias prompt is auto-translated by Claude on demand.
Recording — tap to stop, long-press to cancel
Microphone permission denied. Grant it in Android settings to use voice input.
Cloud usage — what does this show?
You see an ESTIMATE of how much you spent on Cloud TTS this month. The estimate comes from the server-internal character counter × voice price per 1M.
The value is GROSS — before your $10 AI-Pro credit is applied. As long as gross spend ≤ remaining credit, your credit card is not charged at month\'s end.
The exact figure is in the Google Cloud Console under Billing — this display is just an in-app helper, may differ by a few cents.
⚠ Estimated real cost at month\'s end: %1$s (over the credit)
✓ Within credit — credit card will not be charged.
Search the web for up-to-date info.
💡 Tip: The TTS key can be the same AI Studio key as the one for images. If you don\'t enter one here, the server automatically uses the image key (see "Images" section below).
This key is already in the pool.
Welcome! Add your first server profile to get started.
